title: Spilferithimus-narlamos
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Spilfer.jpg]] |name=Spilferithimus-narlamos |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Squib/Legends|Squib]][[Scavenger Hunt (adventure)|''Scavenger Hunt'']] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height=0.97 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Blue]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Squib Polyanarchy]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Us Squibs learn real good Basic. Study 'em up lots from examining plenty good garbage, you bet.|Spilferithimus-narlamos|}} '''Spilferithimus-narlamos''', often called '''Spilfer''' for short, was a [[Color/Legends|blue]]-[[Hair/Legends|furred]] [[Squib/Legends|Squib]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]]—a status that allowed him to sign agreements on behalf of his entire [[Species/Legends|species]]. Sometime between [[0 ABY/Legends|0]] and [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], the Squib [[King/Legends|King]], [[Ebareebaveebeedee]], charged the diplomat with leading a group of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[operative]]s through the [[Paradise system/Legends|Paradise system]] to obtain a "quest object" for the Squibs. In exchange, the King agreed to provide the coordinates of the [[Imperial Armored Transport]] [[Elusive (transport)|''Elusive'']], which had information that could compromise Rebel operations in several [[Sector/Legends|sectors]]. Spilferithimus-narlamos and the Squibs neglected to mention key facts: the [[Star system/Legends|system]] consisted mostly of garbage—including pieces of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] destroyed [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]]—the "quest object" was a [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projector]] that kept the system from complete collapse, and the Squibs' hereditary rivals, the [[Ugor/Legends|Ugors]], controlled Paradise and considered the quest-object to be [[Prime Mover|part]] of [[Holiest of Holies|their holiest shrine]]. Spilferithimus-narlamos disguised himself to sneak past the [[Ugor/Legends|Ugor]] officials who greeted the Rebel [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. However, he was discovered by the Ugor [[Tax Chaplain]] [[GrrKack]], and the Rebels were forced to pay an exorbitant sum for bringing a "[[Demon/Legends|demon]] of a Squib" into the holy system. Nevertheless, Spilferithimus-narlamos led his charges into a large piece of Death Star wreckage, where he filled his many pouches with refuse amassed with a large [[garbage grabber]] until the group reached the "quest object." The Rebels extricated the gravity well projector, slipped it past a band of [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt's]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] who sought the projector for their employer, and brought the loot back to their [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|shuttle]]. However, the removal of the projector threw the delicate [[Gravity/Legends|gravitational]] balance of the system off kilter, turning it into chaotic field of debris. Spilferithimus-narlamos manned the [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle's]] [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]] to deflect dangerous pieces of garbage from striking the vessel as it fought off the miffed Ugors in their [[Black Hole-class Salvage Dreadnought|battle-wagons]] and escaped into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. Back on the Squib [[Momship]], Spilferithimus-narlamos and the Squibs exchanged the coordinates of the ''Elusive'' for the gravity well projector—an item for which they had no other use than to bother their rivals, the Ugors. After more spirited haggling, Ebareebaveebeedee handed over the projector for the Rebels to use in their continued hunt for the ''Elusive'', and he declared a [[Unidentified Squib pact|pact of mutual support]] between the Squibs and the Rebel Alliance, Spilferithimus-narlamos attesting. ==Biography== ===Encountering the Rebel operatives=== {{Quote|There big juju. Grab 'em and get leaving before some pushy Ug show up. He ask plenty awkward questions, you bet.|Spilferithimus-narlamos upon reaching the Ugors' gravity well projector|}} [[File:UgorShip.jpg|thumb|left|Spilferithimus-narlamos was stationed aboard the Squib Momship, ''Thrifty'' (left), when it engaged with Ugor aggressors in the Dankayo system.]] As a member of the diminutive, trash-loving [[Squib/Legends|Squib]] [[Species/Legends|species]], Spilferithimus-narlamos—who went by the nickname Spilfer—traveled with his species' [[Squib Reclamation Fleet|Reclamation Fleet]], collecting garbage jettisoned by [[Starship/Legends|starships]] before they jumped to [[Lightspeed/Legends|lightspeed]]. In the process, he learned [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] from studying examples of that [[Language/Legends|language]] hidden among the detritus they collected. He rose through the ranks of his species' government, the [[Squib Polyanarchy]], to become a full-fledged [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]] with a post aboard the ''[[Thrifty]]'', [[Momship]] of the [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]]. At some point between [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]],[[Scavenger Hunt (adventure)|''Scavenger Hunt'']] takes place at an unspecified point after the destruction of the first [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], which occurred in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' and before the assignment of [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]] to [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]], which occurred in [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]] according to ''The New Essential Chronology''. the ''Thrifty'' traveled to the [[Dankayo system/Legends|Dankayo system]] to collect the garbage jettisoned from the [[Elusive (transport)|''Elusive'']], an [[Imperial Armored Transport]] they had tracked there through the work of a Squib [[Spy/Legends|spy]] known as [[Dono (Squib)|Dono]], who was embedded on board. In the process, they surveyed a [[Battle of Dankayo|battle]] between the forces of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. The Squibs noted both that the Rebels damaged the ''Elusive'' before it leapt to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], as well as the fact that the Rebels attempted to scan the starship's trajectory to pursue it to its next destination. The Squibs began to gather the ''Elusive''{{'s}} refuse with [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]], but they were interrupted when [[Black Hole-class Salvage Dreadnought|Salvage Dreadnoughts]] flown by the Squibs' hereditary enemies, the [[Ugor/Legends|Ugors]], arrived and opened fire on both the Squibs and the Rebels. The Ugor vessels gathered as much garbage as possible while fending off the other vessels before escaping to hyperspace. With calm restored, the Squibs smelled an opportunity to cause mayhem for their foes, the Ugors: The Rebels' scanning proved they wanted to follow the ''Elusive'' to its next destination, and Dono would soon broadcast the coordinates of the ''Elusive'' so the Reclamation Fleet could locate it again. The Squibs could sell the coordinates to the Rebels in exchange for the outsiders running a little errand: travel to the Ugors' home, the [[Paradise system/Legends|Paradise system]], steal an important artifact, and throw the entire [[Star system/Legends|star system]] into disarray. Accordingly, when they received Dono's hail on a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]]-band channel, the Squibs rebroadcast it on an open frequency so the Rebels would overhear. The Squib spy mentioned that the ship had established its new destination and that the coordinates would be transmitted soon. The Squibs then hailed the Rebels and invited them aboard the Momship to speak of [[Illustrious Chieftain of Junkyards|Chieftain of All Junkyards]] to bargain for the coordinates. In audience, the Chieftain, [[King/Legends|King]] [[Ebareebaveebeedee]], promised to reveal the whereabouts of the ''Elusive'' provided the Rebels travel to the Paradise system for him to obtain a "quest object"—actually a [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projector]] from the debris of the Empire's [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destroyed]] [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], which was holding the junk-filled expanses of the Paradise system from falling into chaos. The King left the object's identity vague and sent Spilferithimus-narlamos to guide the Rebels to it as the "[[Ambassador/Legends|Special-Ambassador-to-big-guys-who-are-not-Imperials]]." ===Paradise found=== {{Quote|I can't believe my eyes. I dink dey brought us a sacrifice!''"''
''"''I can't believe your eyes either.|GrrKack and Spilferithimus-narlamos, after the Ugor discovers the Squib "sacrifice" aboard the Rebels' ship|}} [[File:SH_Paradise_system.jpg|thumb|right|At the behest of the Squib King, Spilferithimus-narlamos and his Rebel charges traveled to the Ugor-controlled Paradise system.]] Spilferithimus-narlamos boarded the Rebels' ship, a [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|Lambda-class T-4a shuttle]] called the ''[[Bonderium]]''. Despite some difficulty communicating due to his thick accent when speaking Basic, the Squib led the Rebels to a star system known as J21-Z64 to the ship's [[Computer/Legends|computers]], but as Paradise to the Squibs and Ugors. Upon arrival, the Ugors hailed them as religious "pilgrims" and sent a dreadnought to dock with the Rebel ship. Spilferithimus-narlamos, knowing dire consequences would follow should he be discovered, hid. Three Ugors boarded the ship and escorted the Rebels aboard their vessel to collect the proper "ceremonial donation" for permission to move about the system. From hiding, Spilferithimus-narlamos felt an overwhelming urge to participate in the haggling he knew must be transpiring across the [[Docking tube/Legends|cofferdam]]. He thus donned a disguise—a [[Human/Legends|Human]]-sized [[Blast helmet/Legends|blast helmet]] and [[tech-suit]]—and boarded the Ugor vessel. There, he entered a spirited round of bargaining with the Ugors, getting the officials to let the outsiders into their system for a mere twelve [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] total. The Ugor [[Tax Chaplain]] [[GrrKack]] removed his [[Glove/Legends|glove]] to shake on the deal, but Spilferithimus-narlamos recoiled at the sight of the [[Limb/Legends|pseudopod]]. The appendage morphed into three [[Eye/Legends|eyestalks]], which allowed GrrKack to recognize the negotiator as a Squib. The Ugors decried the Squib as the "veritable pseudopod of the [[Anti-Prophet]]" and threatened to sacrifice him on the altar of the [[Angel of Taxations and Imports]]. Fortunately for the Squib, the Rebels quickly bargained for his life, securing passage for Spilferithimus-narlamos by paying a hefty "exorcism fee" and by cooking a gourmet feast for the Ugors based on directions from [[L9-G8]], a [[Cooking droid/Legends|cooking droid]] aboard their vessel. Spilferithimus-narlamos guided the Rebels' ship through the debris-strewn system to a burnt-out hulk of debris from the Empire's destroyed Death Star, pronouncing them arrived at the location of the "quest-object." Upon the Rebels' shock at having to haul away such a massive hunk of scrap, he clarified that the object of their quest was actually something held ''within'' the hulk. Despite a large number of Ugor ships guarding the wreckage—to the natives, considered their [[Holiest of Holies]]—the Rebel ship slipped past the patrols and landed on the wreckage. [[File:Gravity_well_projector.jpg|thumb|left|Spilferithimus-narlamos led a group of Rebels to recover a gravity well projector from the wreckage of the first Death Star.]] The hulk still had artificial [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] but no breathable [[Atmosphere/Legends|air]]. Spilferithimus-narlamos and the others donned [[Breath mask/Legends|breath masks]], and the Squib led them through the twisting remnants of corridors and chambers. Along the way, the Squib diplomat filled his pouches with huge amounts of refuse, growing suspicious that the Rebels allowed him to do so without competing with him for the choicest pieces. Spilferithimus-narlamos led the group through the remnants of a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] locker room, helped the Rebels survive an encounter with rogue, power-seeking automata known as [[Void Droid]]s, and led them through the personal chambers of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] who had been stationed aboard the Death Star before its destruction.[[Star Wars: A New Hope (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope'' novelization]] The group eventually reached the remnants of a [[Garbage compactor/Legends|garbage masher]]. There, Spilferithimus-narlamos was filled with glee to see so much garbage, and he busied himself retrieving items with his [[garbage grabber]], filling his collection pouches. A [[Dianoga/Legends|dianoga]] in the [[Water/Legends|water]] wrapped a [[Limb/Legends|tentacle]] around the Squib and attempted to strangle him. The Rebels at first dismissed Spilferithimus-narlamos's screams as excitement over the detritus there, but they eventually realized the danger and saved the ambassador. Spilferithimus-narlamos then led the group to the center of the hulk, where the [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projector]] sat, powered by Ugorian [[Power plant/Legends|power plant]]s. The Squib suggested they take it quickly before the Ugors showed up. He then took out a long tool and struck the projector, triggering an alarm. Spilferithimus-narlamos then closed his [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and chose a dial at random to turn, only to have the knob come off in his hand. Nevertheless, the siren ceased, and a green light ignited within the device. Spilferithimus-narlamos pocketed the knob. He led the Rebels back toward their ship as the Paradise system began to collapse into chaos, robbed of the one thing that had brought order to it. Before they could board their ship, the Squib and the Rebels came under attack by [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] led by a Human woman named [[Teehl]], who worked for [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], a [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] who also wanted the projector. The Rebels talked the droid L9-G8 into maneuvering the ship close by and using its [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] to latch onto the gravity well projector and thus bring it—along with the Rebels holding onto it—into the vessel and away from the assailants. The Rebels fled the Paradise system, which was now devolving into an obstacle course after being robbed of its artificial focus. Spilferithimus-narlamos manned the ship's tractor beams to deflect junk that threatened the vessel. Nevertheless, four Ugor dreadnoughts attacked the group, angered by the theft of the projector, which to them was a religious artifact known as the [[Prime Mover]]. The Rebels avoided destruction and prepared to jump to hyperspace when a fleet of Squib [[needle ship]]s—including the Momship, ''Thrifty''—arrived and began swiping up garbage. The Ugors retreated. ===Aftermath=== King Ebareebaveebeedee hailed the Rebels and invited them aboard the Momship. There, he accepted the gravity well projector and revealed to the Rebels the coordinates of the ''Elusive'', now undergoing repairs at the [[Tolan Navy Yards]] and scheduled to depart for the [[Imperial Intelligence Operations Center]] on the planet [[Coronar]]. The King also agreed to let the Rebels have the gravity well projector in exchange for salvage rights to the soon-to-be-destroyed ''Elusive''. In closing, he gave the Rebels a sealed [[Datacard/Legends|datapack]] for them to open only after their mission was completed. The Rebels used the projector to yank the ''Elusive'' from hyperspace and destroy it. When they opened the King's parting gift, it broadcast a gigantic [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] of the Squib leader, who declared a [[Unidentified Squib pact|pact of mutual support]] between the Squib Polyanarchy and the Alliance to Restore the Republic, Spilferithimus-narlamos attesting to the agreement. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Ahhh. Paradise!|Spilferithimus-narlamos upon arrival at the Paradise system|}} [[File:Squibgg4.jpg|thumb|right|upright|Spilferithimus-narlamos used a small blaster and a garbage grabber, much like the Squib pictured here.]] Spilferithimus-narlamos was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] Squib with [[Color/Legends|blue]] [[Hair/Legends|fur]] and a dark [[Nose/Legends|nose]]. During his mission to the Paradise system, sometime between 0 and 2 ABY, Spilferithimus-narlamos was still young for a Squib. At 0.97 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall, he was somewhat short for his species''[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races]]'' and particularly diminutive by Human standards. As such, Spilferithimus-narlamos was fairly weak physically. Nevertheless, he made up for his diminutive nature with agility, alertness, and reflexes that allowed him to dodge harmful attacks. He possessed skill at bargaining, fast-talking, and searching, and was a masterful operator of tractor beams. As a diplomat, Spilferithimus-narlamos spoke Basic, although he thought his level of fluency higher than it was, unaware of the comprehension problems caused by his thick accent. Spilferithimus-narlamos wanted to earn esteem from his fellow Squibs by "dealing the good deal." In his capacity as a diplomat, he possessed the power to sign treaties that were binding to the entire Squib species. He wore a formal [[Fizz-Pop Cap (with foil cluster)]] as the sign of his office. Spilferithimus-narlamos covered his body in pouches and pockets—only "clothing" in a loose sense of the term—that accorded with his species' sense of fashion. These bags and satchels allowed the Squib to exercise his penchant for collecting random pieces of junk that caught his eye; in fact, the Squib experienced bliss at the sight of large amounts of garbage, and was highly suspicious of anyone who did not share his glee at collecting it. He had no compunctions about begging people to give him items they possessed, and he expressed affection for anyone who succumbed to such pestering. The diplomat kept a garbage grabber slung across his right shoulder to aid him in this task: although the device appeared menacing, it merely allowed him to retrieve distant pieces of detritus with its built-in tractor beam.''[[Rookies: Rendezvous]]'' Spilferithimus-narlamos carried a tiny [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] plated in [[Tin/Legends|tin]] for personal defense; the weapon resembled a toy, and was ineffective beyond short range. Although he considered the Ugor-controlled Paradise system to be a paradise in every sense of the word, he shared his species' fundamental intolerance toward the Ugors. Indeed, Spilferithimus-narlamos recoiled at the mere idea of touching a member of the unicellular species. ==Behind the scenes== Author [[Brad Freeman]] introduced Spilferithimus-narlamos in [[Scavenger Hunt (adventure)|''Scavenger Hunt'']], an adventure published in [[1989]] for the [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|first edition]] of [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. His portrait was drawn by artist [[John Robinson]]. In the adventure, Spilferithimus-narlamos is a non-player character controlled by the gamemaster. As such, the character adds comic relief to the adventure, such as when his poor language skills in Basic lead the player characters to believe they must cart away an enormous chunk of the destroyed Death Star rather than the much smaller gravity well projector stored within it. Including him in the Rebel characters' party also allows the gamemaster to provide clues and suggestions through the Squib in situations where the players have difficulty formulating plans to overcome the adventure's obstacles. For instance, during their escape from the Death Star hulk, the Squib suggests that the Rebels grab hold of the gravity well projector and then get their ship [[Droid/Legends|droid]] to pull it into the starship via a tractor beam should none of them come up with the idea first. Later, if the player characters cannot figure out how to successfully ambush the ''Elusive'' after learning of its location from the Squibs, Spilferithimus-narlamos suggests using the gravity well projector to yank it out of hyperspace. The events recounted above assume a best-case outcome for the player characters. At certain points in the adventure, however, the adventure can become more difficult if certain events befall Spilferithimus-narlamos. For example, if the Ugors discover Spilferithimus-narlamos aboard the Rebel [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] when they first board the vessel in the Paradise system, they take him [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]] as an "enemy of Ugor" and force the Rebels to leave the system, permanently banished—and thus fail their mission. Later, if the Rebels prevent Spilferithimus-narlamos from striking the gravity well projector, the device remains firmly ensconced in the Ugors' power plant and proves much more difficult to remove. Depending on where the Rebel operatives land their shuttle on the Death Star wreckage, the order of encounters within the hulk changes. The sequence above reflects the order in which the encounters appear in the published version of the adventure. In the [[2003]] sourcebook ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'', for [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'', Spilferithimus-narlamos is an example name for the Squib species.''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'' ==Appearances== *[[Scavenger Hunt (adventure)|''Scavenger Hunt'']] {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Diplomats]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Squibs]]
